description_long: This is a cylindrical water tower elevated on a sturdy metal framework, featuring a conical roof and cross-braced supports. The tank itself is dark, weathered metal, with the word 'WASSER' visibly stenciled on one side, indicating its function as a water storage unit. The supporting structure consists of four main vertical beams connected by diagonal and horizontal braces, giving it an industrial, utilitarian appearance. The roof is slightly pointed, with a small finial at the top, and the tank appears to be riveted or welded together. There are no visible windows, doors, or ladders, emphasizing its purely functional design. The overall look is reminiscent of early 20th-century urban or railway water towers commonly found in industrial districts.
description: A dark, cylindrical metal water tower with a conical roof, elevated on a cross-braced metal frame and marked with 'WASSER' on one side. Its industrial design is utilitarian and robust.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical tank, conical roof, and cross-braced metal supports.
description_view_1: Side view with the tank and frame visible, no text or markings apparent.
description_view_2: View with 'WASSER' text clearly visible on the tank, highlighting its function.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, tank and frame visible, no text or markings apparent.
